records.ety: [OF. joli, jolif, joyful, merry, F. joli pretty; of Scand. origin, akin to E. yule; cf. Icel. jōl yule, Christmas feast. See Yule.]

defs: Full of life and mirth; jovial; joyous; merry; mirthful.
quotes: Like a jolly troop of huntsmen.
“A jolly place,” said he, “in times of old! But something ails it now: the spot is cursed.”

defs: Expressing mirth, or inspiring it; exciting mirth and gayety.
quotes: And with his jolly pipe delights the groves.
Their jolly notes they chanted loud and clear.

defs: Of fine appearance; handsome; excellent; lively; agreeable; pleasant.
quotes: Full jolly knight he seemed, and fair did sit.
The coachman is swelled into jolly dimensions.

defs: To cause to be jolly; to make good-natured; to encourage to feel pleasant or cheerful; -- often implying an insincere or bantering spirit; hence, to poke fun at.
quotes: We want you to jolly them up a bit.
At noon we lunched at the tail of the ambulance, and gently “jollied” the doctor's topography.

defs: A marine in the English navy.
quotes: I'm a Jolly -- 'Er Majesty's Jolly -- soldier an' sailor too!
